instruction: show me total sales by product for August
output:
august_sales = df[(df['action_time'] >= '2024-08-01') & (df['action_time'] <= '2024-08-31')]
august_product_sales = august_sales.groupby('product')['total'].sum().reset_index()
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.bar(august_product_sales['product'], august_product_sales['total'], color=medium)
ax.set_xlabel('Product', font_properties=manjari_bold)
ax.set_ylabel('Total Sales', font_properties=manjari_bold)
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
ax.set_title('Total Sales by Product for August', font_properties=manjari_bold)
plt.show()

instruction: give me a table of total quantities sold by machine type in July
output:
july_sales = df[(df['action_time'] >= '2024-07-01') & (df['action_time'] <= '2024-07-31')]
machine_sales_july = july_sales.groupby('product')['quantity'].sum().reset_index()
machine_sales_july.columns = ['Product', 'Total Quantities Sold']
machine_sales_july

instruction: show me the distribution of sales per product as a pie chart
output:
product_sales = df.groupby('product')['total'].sum().reset_index()
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.pie(product_sales['total'], labels=product_sales['product'], autopct='%1.1f%%', startangle=90)
ax.set_title('Sales Distribution by Product', font_properties=manjari_bold)
plt.show()

instruction: show me total photo sales over time
output:
photo_sales = df[df['type'] == 'Photo'].groupby('Date')['total'].sum().reset_index()
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.plot(photo_sales['Date'], photo_sales['total'], color=medium)
ax.set_xlabel('Date', font_properties=manjari_bold)
ax.set_ylabel('Total Photo Sales', font_properties=manjari_bold)
plt.xticks(rotation=90)
ax.set_title('Total Photo Sales Over Time', font_properties=manjari_bold)
plt.show()

instruction: show me the total sales per employee in a bar chart
output:
employee_sales = df.groupby('staff')['total'].sum().reset_index()
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.bar(employee_sales['staff'], employee_sales['total'], color=medium)
ax.set_xlabel('Employee', font_properties=manjari_bold)
ax.set_ylabel('Total Sales', font_properties=manjari_bold)
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
ax.set_title('Total Sales by Employee', font_properties=manjari_bold)
plt.show()

instruction: give me a table of total albums sold by employee
output:
album_sales = df[df['type'] == 'Photo'].groupby('staff')['quantity'].sum().reset_index()
album_sales.columns = ['Employee', 'Total Albums Sold']
album_sales

instruction: show me the machine sales per day for July
output:
july_sales = df[(df['action_time'] >= '2024-07-01') & (df['action_time'] <= '2024-07-31')]
machine_sales_july = july_sales.groupby('Date')['total'].sum().reset_index()
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.plot(machine_sales_july['Date'], machine_sales_july['total'], color=medium)
ax.set_xlabel('Date', font_properties=manjari_bold)
ax.set_ylabel('Total Sales', font_properties=manjari_bold)
plt.xticks(rotation=90)
ax.set_title('Machine Sales per Day for July', font_properties=manjari_bold)
plt.show()
